```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
};

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations(const std::vector<Product>& products) {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    // Handle error
    return {};
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  // Read the products from the database
  auto read_result = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Product>>(conn);
  if (!read_result) {
    // Handle error
    return {};
  }
  auto products_from_db = *read_result;

  // Compute the mathematical operations for each product
  std::vector<MathAnalysis> analysis;
  for (const auto& product : products_from_db) {
    MathAnalysis ma;
    ma.product_id = product.product_id;
    ma.name = product.name;
    ma.abs_price = std::abs(product.price);
    ma.ceil_price = std::ceil(product.price);
    ma.floor_price = std::floor(product.price);
    ma.rounded_price = std::round(product.price * 100) / 100; // Round to two decimal places
    ma.exp_discount = std::exp(product.discount_rate / 100);
    ma.ln_price = std::log(product.price);
    ma.log2_quantity = std::log2(product.quantity);
    ma.sin_discount = std::sin(product.discount_rate);
    ma.cos_discount = std::cos(product.discount_rate);
    ma.tan_discount = std::tan(product.discount_rate);
    ma.sqrt_quantity = std::sqrt(product.quantity);
    analysis.push_back(ma);
  }

  return analysis;
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis
```

Now, let's write the code.

We'll use the following aliases for the tables:
- Department: alias "t1"
- Employee: alias "t2"

We'll use the column literals with these aliases.

We'll build the queries for each join type.

### Code:

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_department_employees {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string department_name;
  std::string location;
};

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
  double salary;
};

struct JoinResult {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::optional<std::string> first_name;
  std::optional<std::string> last_name;
  std::optional<double> salary;
};

struct JoinAnalysis {
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
};

JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees_impl(const std::vector<Department>& depts, const std::vector<Employee>& emps) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(":memory:");
  if (!conn_result) {
    // Handle error? We'll return an empty analysis.
    return JoinAnalysis{};
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  // Create tables
  auto create_dept = sqlgen::create_table<Department>;
  auto create_emp = sqlgen::create_table<Employee>;

  // Execute the create table statements
  conn.execute(create_dept.to_sql()).value();
  conn.execute(create_emp.to_sql()).value();

  // Insert data
  conn.write(depts.begin(), depts.end()).value();
  conn.write(emps.begin(), emps.end()).value();

  // Define the queries for each join
  auto inner_query = sqlgen::select_from<Department, "t1">("department_name"_t1, "first_name"_t2, "last_name"_t2, "salary"_t2)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<Employee, "t2">("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);

  auto left_query = sqlgen::select_from<Employee, "t1">("first_name"_t1, "last_name"_t1, "salary"_t1, "department_name"_t2)
      | sqlgen::left_join<Department, "t2">("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);

  auto right_query = sqlgen::select_from<Employee, "t1">("first_name"_t1, "last_name"_t1, "salary"_t1, "department_name"_t2)
      | sqlgen::right_join<Department, "t2">("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);

  auto full_query = sqlgen::select_from<Employee, "t1">("first_name"_t1, "last_name"_t1, "salary"_t1, "department_name"_t2)
      | sqlgen::full_join<Department, "t2">("department_id"_t1 == "department_id"_t2);

  // Execute the queries and collect results
  auto inner_dynamic = transpilation::to_sql(inner_query);
  auto inner_select = rfl::get<dynamic::SelectFrom>(inner_dynamic);
  auto inner_result = conn.read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(inner_select).value();

  auto left_dynamic = transpilation::to_sql(left_query);
  auto left_select = rfl::get<dynamic::SelectFrom>(left_dynamic);
  auto left_result = conn.read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(left_select).value();

  auto right_dynamic = transpilation::to_sql(right_query);
  auto right_select = rfl::get<dynamic::SelectFrom>(right_dynamic);
  auto right_result = conn.read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(right_select).value();

  auto full_dynamic = transpilation::to_sql(full_query);
  auto full_select = rfl::get<dynamic::SelectFrom>(full_dynamic);
  auto full_result = conn.read<std::vector<JoinResult>>(full_select).value();

  // Build the analysis
  JoinAnalysis analysis;
  analysis.inner_join_results = std::move(inner_result);
  analysis.left_join_results = std::move(left_result);
  analysis.right_join_results = std::move(right_result);
  analysis.full_join_results = std::move(full_result);

  return analysis;
}

}  // namespace analyze_department_employees
```

   std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count) {
     // Connect to the database
     auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

     // Read all products and sales
     auto products = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Product>>(conn);
     auto sales = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Sales>>(conn);

     // Create a map from product_id to product
     std::map<int, Product> product_map;
     for (const auto& p : products) {
         product_map[p.product_id] = p;
     }

     // Compute total sales for each product
     std::map<int, double> total_sales;
     for (const auto& s : sales) {
         auto it = product_map.find(s.product_id);
         if (it != product_map.end()) {
             total_sales[s.product_id] += it->second.price * s.quantity;
         }
     }

     // Sort products by total sales in descending order
     std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> sorted_products;
     for (const auto& [id, ts] : total_sales) {
         sorted_products.emplace_back(id, ts);
     }
     std::sort(sorted_products.begin(), sorted_products.end(),
                 [](const auto& a, const auto& b) { return a.second > b.second; });

     // Take the top limit_count products
     std::vector<TopProduct> result;
     for (size_t i = 0; i < limit_count && i < sorted_products.size(); ++i) {
         auto id = sorted_products[i].first;
         auto ts = sorted_products[i].second;
         auto product = product_map[id];
         result.push_back({product.product_id, product.product_name, product.category, ts});
     }

     return result;
   }
